key_achievements: HDRI environment integration (city.exr for day, night.exr for night scenes)
PBR material pipeline established (metallic car paint, glass, asphalt, rubber, chrome)
Forensic overlay system deployed (scale bar, disclaimer text, evidence markers, north arrow)
Multi-camera forensic setup per scene (BirdEye, DriverPOV, WideAngle + scene-specific variants)
Night scene lighting recipe implemented (3x street spots 3000W, flood 2000W, headlights 800W)
Road procedural texture with bump map overlay
Environmental detail (utility poles, curbs, street furniture)
AgX color management with Punchy look applied
Impact deformation setup (displace modifiers on collision vehicles)
DriverPOV camera positioning optimized across day scenes (avg 81 pixel score)
Score improvement: v12 (63.0) → v13.1 (72.9) = +9.9 point gain
remaining_problems: Vehicle geometry severely limited (~500-1100 vertices per vehicle) — far below photorealistic standards
No visible mesh deformation (displace modifier applied but effects are subtle/imperceptible)
Pedestrian figures are basic sphere markers, not realistic human models
No animated sequences (all renders static, no motion blur or temporal continuity)
Missing interior dashboard and steering wheel geometry for DriverPOV realism
BirdEye cameras show mostly road surface with small, distant objects
Edge detail scorer consistently flags absence of meaningful edge geometry
HDRI uses built-in low-resolution city.exr (not from Polyhaven high-quality library)
Night scene (Scene 4) underperforms day scenes by 4.5 points (lighting still insufficient)
SecurityCam view in parking night lacks typical fisheye/wide distortion characteristics

next_priorities.task: Replace GLB vehicle imports with higher-poly models (10k+ vertices minimum)
next_priorities.impact: Critical — vehicle geometry is the primary blocker for forensic plausibility

next_priorities.task: Implement realistic pedestrian models (human figure rigs or at least detailed static meshes)
next_priorities.impact: High — pedestrian presence is essential for Scene 2 forensic accuracy

next_priorities.task: Download and implement Polyhaven HDRI for day/night cycles
next_priorities.impact: Medium-High — significantly improves environment realism and lighting accuracy

next_priorities.task: Add interior cabin geometry to vehicles (dashboard, steering wheel, seats)
Implement animated crash sequences with motion blur and temporal anti-aliasing
next_priorities.impact: Medium — unlocks cinematic presentation tier gains

next_priorities.task: Apply visible impact deformation (enhance displace strength or use sculpt-baked normal maps)
Add SecurityCam fisheye distortion and typical low-light camera artifacts

forensic_clarity_notes: Score 7.1/10 reflects strong overlay presence (scale bar, disclaimers, markers) but undermined by basic vehicle geometry and sphere pedestrian stand-ins. Evidence spatial relationships are clear but geometric realism is insufficient for court-quality forensic visualization.
physical_plausibility_notes: Score 6.7/10 reflects good material setup (PBR workflow) and lighting foundation, but hampered by low-poly vehicle models and subtle deformation effects. HDRI and color grading are solid, but geometry density is the limiting factor.
cinematic_presentation_notes: Score 7.3/10 reflects excellent camera composition and AgX+Punchy color grading, but held back by static-only renders (no motion blur or animation). Multi-camera setup shows strong directorial intent across all scenes.
conclusion: v13.1 represents a significant checkpoint with +9.9 point improvement from v12. Overlay and material infrastructure is solid. Primary blocker for next tier (80+) is geometry fidelity — vehicle and pedestrian models must be substantially upgraded. HDRI quality and animation capability are secondary priorities.
